definition: a bush is a kind of plant that is like a tree, but smaller
example: thereвАЩs something moving around in the bushes

definition: if you chase someone or something, you go after them very quickly because you want to catch them. If you chase them away, you run towards them very quickly because you want to frighten them and make them go away
example: I chased the crows away
police gave chase but he got away down a side street

definition: if you circle, or circle something, you move round and round somewhere
example: the plane had to circle the airport for an hour before we could land
we drove round in circles for ages

definition: a cliff is an area of land which has an extremely steep side that goes down to the sea or a river
example: theyвАЩre circling in the sky above the cliff

definition: when you crawl, you move along the ground on your hands and knees, or you move along very slowly. You also say that insects crawl, because their bodies are so close to the ground
example: babies learn to crawl at about six months old
traffic was moving at a crawl (very slowly) through the city centre

definition: a cry is a sound made by a person or animal when they are in pain or trying to get help by attracting someoneвАЩs attention
example: a faint cry came from the living room
the wounded men lay groaning and crying for help

definition: if something disappears, it goes somewhere where it cannot be seen or it stops existing
example: we waved goodbye and watched the train disappear into the distance
you should report the disappearance to the police

definition: the distance between two things is the amount of space between them. If something is in the distance, you can see it or hear it, but it is a long way from where you are
example: can you hear that noise in the distance?
he heard the distant sound of the motorway (the sound was coming from a long way away)

definition: the fire service is the organisation that provides equipment and people who help to put out fires in homes, offices, factories etc. In Britain, it is often called the fire brigade. The people who work to put out fires are called firefighters. The vehicles they use to carry their equipment and take them to fires are fire engines. The building where they work when they are not fighting fires is called a fire station
example: we had to call the fire service

definition: to get someone or something out of a place where they are stuck means to succeed in releasing them
example: I got stuck in the back seat and couldnвАЩt get out

definition: grass is a low green plant that grows all over the ground. It is made up of thin pointed green leaves
example: the snake disappeared in the long grass
a quiet, grassy lane led up to the church

definition: if you leap somewhere, you move there very quickly, by jumping or running a short distance
example: we saw something leap out of the water
with a huge leap he made it to the other side of the stream

definition: if you lock someone out of somewhere, you prevent them from being able to get in by locking the doors. If you lock yourself out, you accidentally let a door lock when you do not have the key with you, so that you cannot get back in
example: do you remember when you locked yourself out of your flat?

definition: if you race somewhere, you go there very fast
example: the rabbit raced across the road

definition: a seed is a small part of a plant that grows into a new plant when it is in the soil. Birds and other animals eat seeds as food
example: planting carrots is done by sowing seed

definition: altitude is a measurement of the height of something above the level of the sea
example: at this altitude the lack of oxygen can cause confusion

definition: an amateur is someone who takes part in an activity for fun and not for money
example: he played golf as an amateur until he was 22, when he turned professional
a group of amateur cyclists followed the Tour de France route

definition: the authorities are official institutions such as central and local government who are able to make laws and decide how a country, town, or city should be run
example: the authorities in Nepal refused to let him enter their territory

definition: a barrier is a physical object that blocks a road or path and stops you getting past. You can refer to any problem that stops you doing what you want to do as a barrier
example: wonвАЩt the language be a barrier?

definition: a concept is an idea about something. If you have no concept of something, you donвАЩt really know much about it and donвАЩt understand it properly
example: they have no proper concept of the risks
do you have any conception of what itвАЩs like to go hungry every day?

definition: confusion is a state where someone cannot think properly, because there are too many complicated things to consider, or because their brain is affected by something that is stopping it working properly
example: at this altitude, the lack of oxygen can cause confusion
it will just confuse her if you start speaking French
he became confused and frightened when he saw the soldiers
the whole process was very complicated and confusing

definition: if you have determination, you do not let anything stop you from achieving your goals and doing what you want to do
example: he showed amazing strength and determination
he was determined to keep the news secret

definition: a disability is a serious physical condition that prevents someone from using all their body completely or easily
example: Stephen Hawking overcame a serious disability to become a famous scientist

definition: someone who is disabled cannot use all their body completely or easily because of illness, injury etc
example: you have to overcome many barriers when youвАЩre disabled

definition: to disguise yourself means to change your appearance so that people will not recognise you because you look very different
example: he managed to enter Tibet by disguising himself as a monk
he wore a disguise

definition: if something drives you, it gives you very strong reasons to want to do something or achieve something
example: we were driven by hunger
he was a driven man, but he died before he could realise his ambition

definition: if someone drowns, they die by being under water for too long and being unable to breathe any air
example: I was really scared of drowning

definition: if something eases or eases off, it becomes less severe
example: the pain was beginning to ease

definition: to establish something means to create it or build it so that it will last for a long time
example: camp 2 had been established by a previous expedition
the first commercial establishments here opened in 1905

definition: fear is a feeling you have that stops you doing something because you think you are in danger or that something bad will happen to you
example: I finally overcame my fear and swam in the sea
I fear that he will never come home

definition: if you get through an experience or period of time that is very difficult, you succeed in reaching the end of it
example: the race was tough, but I managed to get through it and finish

definition: if something gives you a particular impression, it makes you have a particular feeling about it or form a particular opinion about it
example: wrong/false impression

definition: if you are on the way to doing something, you have made good progress and it is likely that you will succeed in finishing it
example: IвАЩm well on the way to reaching my goal
collocations: be (well) on the way to doing something

definition: if you overcome a problem or difficulty, you succeed in doing what you want to do by achieving something that was difficult or surviving a dangerous situation
example: you have to overcome many barriers when youвАЩre disabled

definition: oxygen is a gas that is one of the main gases in the air that we breathe. Oxygen is an element and has the chemical symbol O. It combines with hydrogen to make water, which has the chemical symbol H2O
example: at this altitude, the lack of oxygen can cause confusion

definition: if something happens or is done only partially, it happens or is done a little but not completely
example: he had been shot and could only partially use his left arm
we made only partial progress towards reaching our goal

definition: a peak is the top of a mountain
example: Mont Blanc is the highest peak in Europe

definition: if you feel proud of something, you have a feeling of great satisfaction because you have done something well
example: she was so proud of herself when she passed the exam
Jack walked in proudly holding the fish heвАЩd caught

definition: to publish a book means to have it printed and made available for people to have or buy
example: my bookвАЩs going to be published next year
he signed a book deal with a New York publisher

definition: if you risk something, you put it in a situation where it might be damaged or destroyed
example: if you go too close, you risk catching the disease
he refused to join in, saying the risk was too big
itвАЩs risky to invest all your money in shares

definition: a rope is a thick length of string made by twisting together several lengths of string to make it stronger and thicker
example: he fastened the rope around my body

definition: if you set someone a challenge or target or something similar, you give it to them to do
example: I set myself a target of learning 50 words a week

definition: if you set off, you start a journey. If you set off for somewhere, you start your journey to go to that place
example: Wilson set off up the mountain without a guide

definition: the summit of a mountain is its highest point
example: we needed to reach the summit before dark

definition: territory is land that belongs to a particular country
example: the authorities in Nepal refused to let him enter their territory
it was a period of rapid territorial expansion (when a country was getting control of more land)

definition: something that is tough is very difficult to do successfully. A tough situation is one that is difficult to deal with
example: I ran a marathon last year; it was tough, but I really enjoyed it

definition: civil war is a war fought between groups of people within one country
example: Syria is being torn apart in a civil war

definition: coal is a solid, black substance that is dug out of the ground and burned to provide energy and heat
example: we used to produce a lot of coal in Wales

definition: conflict or a conflict is violent fighting between groups of people or countries
example: conflict is one of the causes of poverty in the region

definition: to construct something such as a road, airport, or other building means to build it
example: there are plans to construct a new bridge
the construction of the road is a threat to wildlife

definition: if you consume something, you eat it or drink it. In economics, to consume goods or energy means to use them and then replace them with more
example: if we consume coal at the current rate, it will last for 200 years
energy consumption has been significantly reduced
the average consumer eats four chocolate bars a week

definition: if you have control of something, you have enough power to decide what happens to it and to stop people doing what they want with it
example: there was a struggle for control of the oil resources
the government controls the oil industry very tightly

definition: controversy is a serious disagreement among a number of people about an important subject
example: thereвАЩs been some controversy about how they drill for oil

definition: controversial remark/comment
example: a highly controversial issue

definition: controversial remark/comment
example: the government controversially decided to withdraw its support for the project

definition: a countryвАЩs currency is the money that is used there. For example, the currency of the USA is the dollar
example: when the price of oil goes up, the countryвАЩs currency also rises

definition: democracy is a system of government in which all the adults in a country are allowed to vote for the people who will represent them in parliament
example: thereвАЩs a strong democracy in Botswana
of those four countries, only Kenya is truly democratic
a democratically elected government
he described himself as a democrat and an anti-royalist

definition: if you drill a hole in something, you use a tool to make a hole in a surface or in the ground. If you drill for oil or gas, you use industrial equipment to make deep holes in order to find oil or gas below the ground
example: they plan to drill 12 wells over the next year
locals donвАЩt want the drilling to take place because they believe it will damage their houses

definition: if you enforce a law or rule, you use your authority to make sure that people obey the law or follow the rule
example: the only nation to enforce the ban has been Canada
Webb had tremendous respect for those in law enforcement (the police)

definition: to exploit something means to use it in a way that gives you an advantage or benefit
example: more should be done to exploit our natural resources
commercial exploitation of wind power is increasing

definition: if you extract a substance such as oil or gas, you get it out of the ground using industrial machinery
example: it gets very expensive to extract oil out of the ground
coal extraction was difficult because of the upper layer of rock

definition: imports are goods that are brought into one country from another country
example: when imports are cheap, local producers canвАЩt compete
in 1985 China imported a record 15 million tons of steel
in 1985 China imported a record 15 million tons of steel

definition: a countryвАЩs infrastructure is all of the important things it needs in order to be able to operate successfully as a country, such as roads, railways, hospitals, power stations etc
example: investment in infrastructure is desperately needed

definition: if there is instability, a situation is likely to change suddenly
example: one of the reasons is regional instability
an unstable economy
economic stability increased
the economy was thought to be stable up until 2008

definition: in a court of law, the judge is the person who is in charge of a trial, who advises the jury, and who decides on the punishment if the prisoner is found guilty
example: the judge ruled that the witness should be allowed to give evidence
the Court of Appeal reversed this judgement (changed it from guilty to not guilty)

definition: to manufacture something means to make it in large quantities in a factory. Goods that are made in a factory are called manufactured goods, and the activity is called
example: the factory used to manufacture tractors
imports of manufactured goods increased last year
manufacturing accounted for 8% of employment within Birmingham in 2012
imports of manufactured goods increased last year

definition: to mine coal, gold, salt etc means to dig it out of the ground using industrial machinery
example: itвАЩs too expensive to mine for coal here
we went on a tour round a salt mine
a coal miner
mining and forestry are the two main industries here

definition: an official is someone who works for a government department or large organisation and who holds a position of authority
example: companies may give вАШpresentsвАЩ to officials to avoid import taxes

definition: raw materials are things such as wool, wood etc in their natural state, before they get processed and turned into manufactured goods
example: our most important raw material is iron

definition: reserves of oil, coal, gas etc are the amounts that are still in the ground somewhere waiting to be dug up
example: Russia has the biggest reserves of natural gas and wood

definition: if something runs out, or if you run out of it, it is all used up and there is no more available
example: gas is likely to run out in 60 years

definition: if the government subsidises something, it provides some of the money for it so that it is cheaper for people to buy or use
example: solar energy is subsidised by the government
the school was fully independent and got no state subsidy

definition: an inspector is someone, usually in a government department, whose job involves making sure that regulations are being followed
example: I had a letter from the tax inspector saying I owed another ¬£400

definition: if something varies, it changes a lot or consists of a lot of different things
example: global prices vary a lot
the work is interesting and varied
different versions are available at varying costs
the interest rate is variable
they offer a wide variety of courses

definition: wealth is ownership of money and property
example: in terms of average wealth per person, India comes 130th in the world
he had married a wealthy businesswoman
he had married a wealthy businesswoman

definition: if something is done wisely, it is done in a sensible way
example: the money was not invested wisely

definition: Adjective  unwise
example: it was a wise decision to buy a bike rather than a car

definition: Adjective  unwise
example: rather unwisely, he started arguing with his boss

definition: a workplace is somewhere such as an office or factory where people work
example: we need more women in the workplace
